artifacts.title: Ambiguous Rotation
artifacts.description: Three rings of dots rotate in 3D with no depth cues — direction is unresolvable. Click to nudge.

artifacts.title: Ant Colony
artifacts.description: Stigmergic ants lay pheromone trails to food and home.

artifacts.title: ASCII Reactive
artifacts.description: Audio-reactive Unicode field. Falls back to synthetic oscillators when mic is off.

artifacts.title: A* Pathfinding
artifacts.description: Interactive A* and Dijkstra pathfinding on a grid. Draw walls, generate mazes, drag endpoints, compare search frontier expansion between heuristic-guided and blind search.

artifacts.title: Audiomountain
artifacts.description: Layered mountain ranges react to pseudo-audio by default and WebAudio analyser input when enabled.

artifacts.title: Aura
artifacts.description: Glowing hue-shifting aura follows your cursor — click to burst, move to paint trails.

artifacts.title: Aurora
artifacts.description: Aurora borealis simulation. Five sinusoidal curtain bands with screen-blend glow passes for depth. Solar wind particles exciting atmospheric oxygen and nitrogen along Earth's magnetic field lines.

artifacts.title: Boids
artifacts.description: Craig Reynolds flocking: separation, alignment, cohesion.

artifacts.title: Brian's Brain
artifacts.description: Brian Silverman's 3-state cellular automaton: cells fire, enter a refractory dying state, then recover. The exactly-2 rule produces gliders, oscillators, and sprawling activity that rarely settles.

artifacts.title: Buddhabrot
artifacts.description: Escape-path orbit density of the Mandelbrot set — each pixel encodes how many escaping trajectories passed through it. Three-channel Nebulabrot mode maps depth ranges to RGB.

artifacts.title: Burning Ship
artifacts.description: The Burning Ship fractal: fold absolute values into escape-time iteration — scroll to zoom, click to center.

artifacts.title: Chainwheel
artifacts.description: Mechanical gear system with teeth, spokes, and chain belts.

artifacts.title: Chinoiserie
artifacts.description: Click to grow flowering branches with falling petals.

artifacts.title: Chladni
artifacts.description: Standing wave nodal patterns: sand collects where a vibrating plate is still.

artifacts.title: Clifford
artifacts.description: Strange attractor x'=sin(ay)+c·cos(ax) — millions of iterated points reveal fractal structure.

artifacts.title: Colorrivers
artifacts.description: Curl noise flow field — thousands of particles bleed saturated color streams across the canvas.

artifacts.title: Constraint
artifacts.description: Rule-based tile placement with adjacency constraints.

artifacts.title: Crystal
artifacts.description: Diffusion-limited aggregation grows a snowflake with 12-fold symmetry.

artifacts.title: Cyclic CA
artifacts.description: Cyclic cellular automaton: n states, Moore neighborhood, threshold — random noise self-organizes into spiral waves.

artifacts.title: Dadras Attractor
artifacts.description: Five-parameter Dadras–Momeni (2009) chaotic attractor. 3D point-trail rendering with auto-rotation, real-time parameter display, and touch/pointer interaction.

artifacts.title: DLA Crystal
artifacts.description: Diffusion-limited aggregation — walkers freeze on contact, growing branching crystal structures.

artifacts.title: DNA Double Helix
artifacts.description: Watson–Crick base pairs rendered as a rotating 3D helix — depth-sorted rungs with A-T and G-C color coding.

artifacts.title: Domain Warping
artifacts.description: Three-level domain warping using fbm(fbm(fbm(p))) — Inigo Quilez technique. WebGL fragment shader with cosine palette cycling.

artifacts.title: Donut
artifacts.description: The classic spinning 3D torus — surface normals mapped to ASCII character brightness.

artifacts.title: FitzHugh-Nagumo
artifacts.description: Excitable media: spiral waves, target waves, and plane pulses from a neural firing model.

artifacts.title: Flow
artifacts.description: Perlin noise vector field with thousands of particle traces.

artifacts.title: Fourier
artifacts.description: Rotating epicycles decompose waveforms into harmonics — watch frequencies build shapes.

artifacts.title: Gradient Descent
artifacts.description: Watch gradient descent, momentum, and Adam navigate loss landscapes: Rosenbrock, Himmelblau, Rastrigin, saddle points, and Beale. Click to place a starting point; compare optimizer behavior on the same terrain.

artifacts.title: Gray-Scott
artifacts.description: Gray-Scott two-chemical model grows coral, spots, and mazes.

artifacts.title: Halftone
artifacts.description: Animated dot grid pulsing with 4-octave noise — four palettes, circle/square geometry toggle.

artifacts.title: Harmonograph
artifacts.description: 19th century pendulum drawing machine — decaying Lissajous traces in sepia ink.

artifacts.title: Hat Monotile
artifacts.description: The 2023 aperiodic monotile (Smith, Myers, Kaplan, Goodman-Strauss). Hierarchical substitution builds H/T/P/F metatiles into supertiles that tile the plane without periodicity. Drag to pan, scroll to zoom, adjust subdivision level and palette.

artifacts.title: Heat
artifacts.description: Drifting thermal sources with real-time diffusion — click to add heat.

artifacts.title: IFS — Iterated Function Systems
artifacts.description: Iterated function systems via the chaos game — Barnsley fern, Sierpiński triangle, dragon curve, and more emerge from four affine transforms and random coin flips.

artifacts.title: Ising Model
artifacts.description: Metropolis-Hastings simulation of a 2D ferromagnet. Drag or use the temperature slider to cross Tc ≈ 2.269 and watch ferromagnetic domains form and dissolve.

artifacts.title: Julia
artifacts.description: z² + c iterated to escape: move mouse to steer c through 8 regions of the complex plane.

artifacts.title: Kaleidoscope
artifacts.description: Mirrored neon wedges with shader-like motion fields — drag to steer symmetry and speed.

artifacts.title: Kelvin-Helmholtz Instability
artifacts.description: Rolling vortices at a fluid shear layer simulated with 300 point vortices and Rosenhead-regularized 2D Biot-Savart. The instability behind clouds and Jupiter's cloud bands.

artifacts.title: Langton
artifacts.description: A single ant turns and flips cells — 10,000 steps of chaos collapse into a repeating highway.

artifacts.title: Life
artifacts.description: Conway's cellular automaton with rainbow-hued cells.

artifacts.title: Lissajous
artifacts.description: Parametric harmonic curves with drifting frequency ratios.

artifacts.title: Logistic Map
artifacts.description: A quadratic recurrence blooms from equilibrium into period-doubling chaos. Drag through the bifurcation diagram to steer r and the starting seed.

artifacts.title: Lorenz
artifacts.description: Deterministic chaos: six particles diverge across the butterfly wings.

artifacts.title: Lotka–Volterra
artifacts.description: Predator-prey dynamics with dual-panel display: time series oscillations and phase portrait. RK4 integration of the classic Lotka–Volterra equations with adjustable parameters and click-to-set initial conditions.

artifacts.title: L-System
artifacts.description: Lindenmayer system fractal trees from string rewriting.

artifacts.title: Mandelbrot
artifacts.description: Click to zoom into infinite fractal complexity — smooth coloring reveals hidden structure.

artifacts.title: Marching Squares
artifacts.description: Animated scalar field sliced into contour levels by the marching squares algorithm.

artifacts.title: Matrix
artifacts.description: Green character cascade (The Matrix, 1999).

artifacts.title: Maurer Rose
artifacts.description: r = sin(n·θ) sampled at d-degree steps: move mouse to steer petal count and step angle.

artifacts.title: Metaballs
artifacts.description: Organic blobs merge and split via implicit isosurface field.

artifacts.title: Mitosis
artifacts.description: Cells grow, split, and drift — click to seed new organisms.

artifacts.title: Moiré Patterns
artifacts.description: Two overlapping periodic grids — lines, circles, or dots — create emergent interference fringes. Drag to offset layers; adjust spacing and rotation angle in real time.

artifacts.title: Molnar
artifacts.description: Vera Molnár: concentric rectangles disrupted by controlled chaos.

artifacts.title: Mondrian
artifacts.description: Recursive binary subdivision produces Mondrian-style compositions — click to regenerate.

artifacts.title: Monte Carlo
artifacts.description: Random points flood a unit square — the circle catches π.

artifacts.title: Nake
artifacts.description: Frieder Nake 1965 — random walk subdivision with Bauhaus palette.

artifacts.title: N-Body Gravity
artifacts.description: Gravitational N-body simulation with Velocity Verlet integration. Five presets: figure-eight three-body orbit (Chenciner-Montgomery 2000), solar system, binary stars with circumbinary planets, random star cluster, and Lagrange points. Click to add mass.

artifacts.title: Newton's Fractal
artifacts.description: Newton's method applied to z⁵ − 1 = 0: each pixel colors by which root iteration converges to; brightness shows convergence speed. The basin boundaries are fractal.

artifacts.title: Noll
artifacts.description: After A. Michael Noll, Bell Labs 1965 — one of the first computer artworks.

artifacts.title: PageRank
artifacts.description: Brin and Page 1998 PageRank algorithm. Power iteration on a directed graph with animated probability flow, convergence tracking, and a random surfer simulation.

artifacts.title: Pendulum
artifacts.description: 24 pendulums at tuned frequencies create mesmerizing phase patterns.

artifacts.title: Penrose Tiling
artifacts.description: Penrose P2 kite-and-dart aperiodic tiling via recursive Robinson triangle subdivision. Pan, zoom, subdivide, cycle color modes.

artifacts.title: Percolation
artifacts.description: Site percolation on a square lattice with union-find cluster detection. Drag or animate probability p to watch the phase transition at the critical threshold p_c ≈ 0.5927, where a spanning cluster first connects top to bottom.

artifacts.title: Phyllotaxis
artifacts.description: Golden angle 137.5° packs 1400 dots into the same spiral pattern sunflowers and pinecones use.

artifacts.title: Quadratic Walk
artifacts.description: Turtle steps rotating by q·π·n² produce closed rosettes — rational q determines the symmetry.

artifacts.title: Rime Sparse
artifacts.description: Petrarch's Canzoniere — 366 fragments in orbit, pulled toward an unreachable centre with every volta.

artifacts.title: Rössler Attractor
artifacts.description: Otto Rössler 1976 strange attractor. 3D ODE integration with auto-rotation, drag control, and preset parameter regimes.

artifacts.title: Rubik
artifacts.description: Procedural isometric Rubik's cube with auto-scrambling colored faces.

artifacts.title: Sand
artifacts.description: Physics-correct particle simulation: sand piles, water flows, fire rises.

artifacts.title: Scandrift
artifacts.description: CRT-like scanline field with phase drift and pointer warping. Tap for a local pulse.

artifacts.title: Schelling Segregation
artifacts.description: Thomas Schelling's 1971 segregation model. Mild individual tolerance thresholds produce strong macro-level clustering. Adjustable tolerance, vacancy rate, and group count with live similarity index.

artifacts.title: Schotter
artifacts.description: Georg Nees: squares with increasing rotational disorder.

artifacts.title: Sierpinski
artifacts.description: Recursive fractal subdivision with color gradients.

artifacts.title: Simony
artifacts.description: Bernoulli site percolation as simony: cells open only if they can pay the coin price p. Below the critical threshold (~0.593), no path opens from earth to heaven. Above it, a giant connected cluster forms and souls can rise.

artifacts.title: Slime Mold
artifacts.description: Physarum polycephalum simulation. 4,000 agents navigate by sampling a shared chemical trail map, self-organizing into vascular networks that connect food sources. Based on Jeff Jones's agent-based model (2010), inspired by Nakagaki's maze-solving experiments (2000).

artifacts.title: Snowflake
artifacts.description: Reiter cellular automaton for hexagonal ice crystal growth. Dendritic, plate, stellar, and sector morphologies emerge from diffusion-limited aggregation on a hex lattice with tunable vapor parameters.

artifacts.title: Sospiri
artifacts.description: Rising particles that never quite arrive — concept: Petrarch, code: Quimbot.

artifacts.title: Springwires
artifacts.description: Verlet-integrated spring network — drag nodes, pin rows, double-click to add nodes.

artifacts.title: Sprott Attractor Family
artifacts.description: Nine strange attractors from Julien Sprott's 1994 systematic search for chaotic 3D ODE systems. RK4 integration, 60k-point trails, interactive 3D rotation.

artifacts.title: Stable Fluids
artifacts.description: Interactive 2D Navier-Stokes fluid simulation based on Jos Stam (1999). Semi-Lagrangian advection, Gauss-Seidel pressure projection, optional vorticity confinement.

artifacts.title: Starfield
artifacts.description: Parallax star tunnel with depth-based scaling and speed.

artifacts.title: Tenprint
artifacts.description: Classic one-liner generative pattern (Commodore 64, 1982).

artifacts.title: Terrain
artifacts.description: Procedural parallax mountain landscape. Six silhouette layers generated with fractional Brownian motion (4 octaves of value noise). Each layer drifts at a different speed; mouse shifts the parallax perspective.

artifacts.title: Thomas
artifacts.description: René Thomas's cyclically symmetric attractor — sin(y)−bx, sin(z)−by, sin(x)−bz — b tunes dissipation from chaos to order.

artifacts.title: Truchet
artifacts.description: Sébastien Truchet, 1704 — quarter-circle arc tiles that self-organize into flowing labyrinths.

artifacts.title: Tunnel
artifacts.description: Hexagonal perspective tunnel with neon glow — move mouse to steer through the vortex.

artifacts.title: Turing
artifacts.description: Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion: coral-like structures emerge from chaos.

artifacts.title: Voronoi
artifacts.description: Drifting seed points carve space into nearest-neighbor cells — click to add, drag to push.

artifacts.title: Waveinterference
artifacts.description: Multiple point sources create rippling interference patterns — drag sources, click to add new.

artifacts.title: Wolfram
artifacts.description: 1D elementary cellular automata: Rule 30 breeds chaos, Rule 110 is Turing-complete.

artifacts.title: de Jong Attractor
artifacts.description: Density-accumulation rendering of Peter de Jong's iterated map. Four parameters — a, b, c, d — shape biomorphic forms from millions of orbit points.

microblogs.title: Nobody Told Them to Flock
microblogs.snippet: 120 boids, three rules, no flock-level state. Click to reset; move mouse to pull the swarm.

microblogs.title: Sunflower Uses 137.5 Degrees
microblogs.snippet: 1,400 dots placed at 137.5° increments, radius scaling as √index. Click to pulse. Move mouse to slow the rotation.

microblogs.title: Stuttgart 1965 First Algorithms
microblogs.snippet: Four coupled pendulums trace a decaying Lissajous figure in sepia ink. Click or tap to reset with new frequencies.

microblogs.title: Lindenmayer Grew a Forest
microblogs.snippet: Seven presets: fractal tree, fern, Koch snowflake, Dragon curve, Sierpiński triangle, bush, Hilbert curve. Space cycles presets; arrow keys adjust depth and angle.

microblogs.title: Space by Proximity Only
microblogs.snippet: 30 drifting seeds carve the canvas by nearest-neighbor ownership. Click to add seeds, drag to push, R to reset.

microblogs.title: Run Gray-Scott Once
microblogs.snippet: Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion. Click to seed new chemical clusters; press space to cycle through five presets (Coral, Mitosis, Spots, Maze, Waves).

microblogs.title: Cayley Never Saw This
microblogs.snippet: Newton's method applied to z⁵ − 1 = 0 over the complex plane. Each pixel's color shows which of the five roots the iteration converged to; brightness reflects convergence speed. Tap or click to...

microblogs.title: Slime Mold Beat Engineers
microblogs.snippet: 4,000 agents navigate by sampling a shared chemical trail map. Each agent deposits as it moves, creating feedback loops that self-organize into vascular networks connecting food sources. Click to...

microblogs.title: Ant Highway No Blueprint
microblogs.snippet: Christopher Langton introduced this system in a 1986 Physica D paper titled "Studying Artificial Life with Cellular Automata." The rules fit in a single sentence: on a white cell, turn right, flip...

microblogs.title: Scramble Without Touching It
microblogs.snippet: Click scramble, then solve. Swipe to change grid size (2×2 → 7×7).

microblogs.title: Julia 1918 Hospital Bed
microblogs.snippet: Gaston Julia published a 199-page memoir on iterated functions from a hospital bed at 25. The paper founded complex dynamics and was ignored for sixty years, until Mandelbrot had a computer.

microblogs.title: Harmonograph: The Parlor Machine, Drawing Decay
microblogs.snippet: Hugh Blackburn was goofing around with Lord Kelvin at Cambridge in 1844. The path traced by a pendulum swinging in two planes at once described a figure-eight, a rosette, a closed ellipse. Victorian drawing rooms called the result a harmonograph.

microblogs.title: DLA: A Random Walk Builds Coral
microblogs.snippet: T. A. Witten and L. M. Sander published a short paper in 1981. A simple random walk that produces fractal geometry with dimension 1.71.

microblogs.title: Chladni: Sand Collects Where Sound Stops
microblogs.snippet: Ernst Chladni made standing wave nodes visible in 1787. Sand collects where the plate does not move.

microblogs.title: Rule 30: Order Wrote the Instructions for Chaos
microblogs.snippet: Wolfram 1983 classified all 256 one-dimensional rules. Rule 30 produces chaos from 11 lines of code. Rule 110 is Turing complete.

microblogs.title: Schotter: Order Falls Apart by Design
microblogs.snippet: Georg Nees fed punched tape into a Zuse Graphomat Z64 plotter in 1968 and produced a grid of squares that begins in perfect alignment and descends, row by row, into rotation and displacement. The machine was precise about imprecision in a way no hand could be.

microblogs.title: 366 Fragments: Building Rime Sparse
microblogs.snippet: Every run begins with 14031327 — Petrarch born 1304, Laura seen 1327. The seed is not arbitrary; it is the work's permanent name. Here is what is underneath the 366 fragments: xorshift32, a harmonic flow field structured as a sonnet, semi-implicit Euler, and an orbit that can never collapse.

microblogs.title: Penrose: Five-Fold Order Without Repetition
microblogs.snippet: Roger Penrose published six tiles in 1974 that forced aperiodicity. Robinson triangle subdivision, golden ratio splits, and a sun decagon seed produce the P2 kite-and-dart tiling.

microblogs.title: Molnár and the Grid That Starts to Slip
microblogs.snippet: Vera Molnár's Interruptions series matters because it shows how little an algorithm needs to do before order becomes expressive. The Morgan Library describes one 1968 drawing as one of the first...

microblogs.title: Truchet: The Combinatorics of a Split Square
microblogs.snippet: Sébastien Truchet split a square along its diagonal in 1704 and enumerated 64 pairwise combinations. Three centuries later Cyril Stanley Smith rescued the work from archival obscurity.

microblogs.title: 1952 Paper Run Live
microblogs.snippet: The Turing Patterns sketch runs on a reaction-diffusion system where most of the visible complexity comes from one compact loop repeated at pixel scale. Each pixel tracks two concentrations, A and...

microblogs.title: Lotka-Volterra: The Adriatic Fish and the Phase Orbit
microblogs.snippet: In 1918 wartime fishing records, Umberto D'Ancona found predatory fish spiking to 36% of Adriatic catches. Vito Volterra wrote two differential equations to explain it. They still do.

microblogs.title: The Boundary That Has No Length
microblogs.snippet: Gaston Julia and Pierre Fatou worked out the mathematics of iterated complex functions independently during the First World War, both sidelined from combat — Julia by a wound that cost him his nose, Fatou by poor health...

microblogs.title: The One-Liner That Wrote a Book
microblogs.snippet: In 1982, someone — the author is unknown and probably unknowable — typed a single line into a Commodore 64 and hit RUN. The program is its own title: 10 PRINT CHR$(205.5+RND(1)); : GOTO 10. It...

microblogs.title: Epicycles Drawing a Square
microblogs.snippet: Tap to switch harmonic families · swipe up/down to change harmonic count

microblogs.title: What Do Four Numbers Do
microblogs.snippet: Click morph to cycle presets · random for chaos

microblogs.title: Lorenz Broke Weather Prediction
microblogs.snippet:
Two visualizations from the gallery that sit at opposite ends of the same idea: systems
      that remember nothing, yet produce structures that look designed.

microblogs.title: Bell Labs 1965 Plotter
microblogs.snippet: In 1965 A. Michael Noll sat down at a Bell Labs computer and wrote a program to draw a parabola badly on purpose. The machine plotted a quadratic curve, then scattered each point with Gaussian...

microblogs.title: Two Slits One Pattern
microblogs.snippet: 24 uncoupled pendulums swinging at arithmetic frequencies. No pendulum talks to any other. The wave pattern is entirely in your eye.

microblogs.title: Lissajous Turned Sound Visible
microblogs.snippet: In 1855, Jules Antoine Lissajous attached tiny mirrors to two tuning forks, aimed a beam of light at each, and let them vibrate at right angles. The reflected light traced closed curves on a...

microblogs.title: A Letter to Scientific American
microblogs.snippet: Peter de Jong submitted four equations as a letter to Scientific American in 1987. The forms they produce look organic — no biology runs here.
